bundles.angle: Your AI coding assistant forgets context every session — the hidden tax on engineering velocity
bundles.summary: Your AI coding assistant forgets context every session. Here's why it costs you 3 hours a week and what to do about it.
bundles.rationale: Strong stat-anchored angle, lands well as carousel + LinkedIn essay. No process/atmospheric element that demands motion.

bundles.sub_topic: Auto-extracting Cursor rules from repo conventions
bundles.angle: Stop writing rules. Extract them from your repo.
bundles.summary: Watch what happens when you stop writing Cursor rules by hand and let the model read your existing codebase patterns instead.
bundles.filming_script: Most teams spend an afternoon writing Cursor rules from scratch. They list out conventions, naming patterns, the way they handle errors. And then those rules drift the moment somebody refactors. There's a cleaner way. You point a model at your repo, ask it to surface the actual patterns you're already using, and let it generate the rules document for you. Last week I ran this on a 40k-line codebase and it caught three conventions I had completely forgotten we used. Took six minutes. The rules file it produced was better than the one I'd been hand-writing for a year. The point isn't automation for automation's sake. The point is that your code already contains the rules. You're just transcribing them.
bundles.rationale: Process showcase with a dramatic before/after — meets the motion criterion. Case-study content_type with strong show-don't-tell element.

bundles.sub_topic: Multimodal prompt structure: files before instructions
bundles.angle: You're sending screenshots to AI but still prompting like it's text-only
bundles.summary: If you're pasting screenshots into Claude or Gemini but still writing the prompt as if it's text-only, you're leaving accuracy on the floor.
bundles.rationale: Tutorial content type — slides handle ordered instructions better than motion. Hard-skip per heuristic.

bundles.sub_topic: Manual prompt vs optimized prompt — 3.2x output quality
bundles.angle: Same task, same model — what optimization actually does
bundles.summary: Same task, same model, 3.2x better output. Here's the side-by-side that shows why structured prompts beat hand-written ones.
bundles.filming_script: Two prompts. Same model. Same task. One was written by someone who thinks they're good at prompting. The other was structured by PromptPerfect. The first one gets a generic answer that's accurate but unusable. The second one returns a structured document with the exact sections we asked for, in the right order, with the right level of detail. Three-point-two times better on our internal rubric. Here's the thing — the person who wrote the first prompt has been doing this for two years. They're not bad at it. The model isn't the bottleneck. The prompt is. When you stop treating prompts like search queries and start treating them like specs, the output quality jumps. Try it on your next task.
bundles.rationale: Comparison reveal with dramatic before/after — textbook motion-demanding concept. BOFU stage benefits from showing the product result visually.

bundles.sub_topic: Context engineering as a discipline, not a trick
bundles.angle: Context engineering is what prompt engineering grew up to be
bundles.summary: Prompt engineering was the diet version. Context engineering is the real discipline — and it's quietly becoming the most valuable skill on every AI team.
bundles.rationale: Opinion content type, founder-essay shape. LinkedIn long-form is the right medium. Hard-skip per heuristic.

bundles.sub_topic: Claude Mythos zero-day bug discovery — emergent capability
bundles.angle: What an emergent capability tells you about prompt quality
bundles.summary: Claude Mythos found a 27-year-old zero-day nobody spotted — what that emergent capability reveals about the prompts you're already writing.
bundles.rationale: News content type — timeliness over motion. Slides and LinkedIn carry it. Hard-skip per heuristic.
